payload.message: System design causes of MoE router load imbalance in inference serving

MoE router load imbalance in inference serving usually comes from a mismatch between routing behavior and real request distribution. A learned router can collapse onto a subset of experts when prompts are skewed, temperature or top-k routing concentrates traffic, or expert specialization drifts during training. The serving layer then amplifies the issue because hot experts become latency bottlenecks while cold experts stay underutilized. Practical mitigations include load-balancing losses during training, capacity constraints, expert parallel scheduling, and runtime admission control so system throughput does not depend on a few overloaded experts.
